note: Tempdoc 594 Move 2 (rung-Gate backstop) — a DeclaredAdaptiveItem chip that asserts a runtime/build FACT (a dimension / accelerator / precision token) MUST use a `fact`-ref projected by the ONE Display-value authority (projectFact, modules/ui-web/src/shell-v0/display/facts.ts), NOT a baked `label` literal. The structural close is Move 1b (the factual form carries a fact-ref, so a wrong literal is unrepresentable); this gate is the backstop that catches an author who hand-types a fact-shaped literal in a chip declaration. Decorative labels (matching no fact-shape) pass untouched — the rule is not 'no static labels', it is 'a static label may not STATE a runtime fact' (594 §4 Move 2 / §9.3).

factShapes.why: an embedding/vector dimension (e.g. 768-d) — derive from core.embed.dim
a CUDA accelerator version (e.g. cuda12) — derive from core.gpu.accel
an accelerator family — derive from core.gpu.accel
a vector precision (e.g. Float32) — derive from core.vector.precision
a vector precision (e.g. fp16) — derive from core.vector.precision
a vector precision (int8) — derive from core.vector.precision
